question: List all books who were published in 2017 whose total pages was beyond the normal range. who were published in 2017 refers to year(publication_date) = '2017'; total pages was beyond the normal range refers to `num_pages` > = '600'
orig_question: List all patients who were born in 1937 whose total cholesterol was beyond the normal range. who were born in 1937 refers to year(birthday) = '1937'; total cholesterol was beyond the normal range refers to `T-CHO` > = '250'
orig_masked: List all [T3] who were [C59] in [V1] whose [C49] was beyond the [C49]. who were [C59] in [V1] refers to year([C59]) = '[V1]'; [C49] was beyond the [C49] refers to `[C49]` > = '[V2]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C59]; [V2] is a value of the column [C49]
evidence: who were published in 2017 refers to year(publication_date) = '2017'; total pages was beyond the normal range refers to `num_pages` > = '600'
orig_evidence: who were born in 1937 refers to year(birthday) = '1937'; total cholesterol was beyond the normal range refers to `T-CHO` > = '250'
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT title FROM book WHERE num_pages >= 600 AND STRFTIME('%Y', publication_date) = '2017'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.ID FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T1.Birthday) = '1937' AND T2.`T-CHO` >= 250

question: Calculate the average payment amount in the 2005 year in the country of France. average payment amount = AVG(amount)= SUM(amount) / COUNT(DISTINCT payments.checkNumber) WHERE country = 'France' and paymentDate = '2005';
orig_question: Calculate the average home team goal in the 2010/2011 season in the country of Poland. average home team goal = AVG(home_team_goal)= SUM(home_team_goal) / COUNT(DISTINCT Match.id) WHERE name = 'Poland' and season = '2010/2011';
orig_masked: Calculate the average [C94] in the [V1] [C108] in the [T1] of [V2]. average [C94] = AVG([C94])= SUM([C94]) / COUNT(DISTINCT [C95]) WHERE [C2] = '[V2]' and [C108] = '[V1]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C108]; [V2] is a value of the column [C2]
evidence: average payment amount = AVG(amount)= SUM(amount) / COUNT(DISTINCT payments.checkNumber) WHERE country = 'France' and paymentDate = '2005';
orig_evidence: average home team goal = AVG(home_team_goal)= SUM(home_team_goal) / COUNT(DISTINCT Match.id) WHERE name = 'Poland' and season = '2010/2011';
gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(t1.amount) AS REAL) / COUNT(t1.checkNumber) FROM payments AS t1 INNER JOIN customers AS t2 ON t1.customerNumber = t2.customerNumber WHERE t2.country = 'France' AND STRFTIME('%Y', t1.paymentDate) = '2005'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(t2.home_team_goal) AS REAL) / COUNT(t2.id) FROM Country AS t1 INNER JOIN Match AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.country_id WHERE t1.name = 'Poland' AND t2.season = '2010/2011'

question: Which patients have dairy allergy with prediabetes diagnosis?  which patients refers to patients.patient; dairy allergy refers to allergies.description = 'Allergy to dairy product' and allergies.patient = patients.patient; prediabetes diagnosis refers to conditions.description = 'Prediabetes' and conditions.patient = patients.patient;
orig_question: Which superheroes have blue eyes with brown hair?  which superheroes refers to superhero_name; blue eyes refers to color = 'Blue' and color.id = eye_colour_id; brown hair refers to color = 'Brown' and color.id = hair_colour_id;
orig_masked: Which [C28] have [V2] [C19] with [V1] [C22]?  which [C28] refers to [C28]; [V2] [C19] refers to [C5] = '[V2]' and [C6] = [C19]; [V1] [C22] refers to [C5] = '[V1]' and [C6] = [C22];; [V1] is a value of the column [C5]; [V2] is a value of the column [C5]
evidence: which patients refers to patients.patient; dairy sensitivities refers to allergies.description = 'Allergy to dairy product' and allergies.patient = patients.patient; prediabetes diagnosis refers to conditions.description = 'Prediabetes' and conditions.patient = patients.patient;
orig_evidence: which superheroes refers to superhero_name; blue eyes refers to color = 'Blue' and color.id = eye_colour_id; brown hair refers to color = 'Brown' and color.id = hair_colour_id;
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.patient FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN allergies AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.patient INNER JOIN conditions AS T3 ON T1.patient = T3.patient WHERE T2.description = 'Allergy to dairy product' AND T3.description = 'Prediabetes'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.superhero_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN colour AS T2 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id INNER JOIN colour AS T3 ON T1.hair_colour_id = T3.id WHERE T2.colour = 'Blue' AND T3.colour = 'Brown'

question: What is the average height of actors born between 1990 and 1995? average height = DIVIDE(SUM(height (inches)), COUNT(id)); actors born between 1990 and 1995 refers to date of birth > = '1990-01-01 00:00:00' AND date of birth < '1996-01-01 00:00:00';
orig_question: What is the average height of players born between 1990 and 1995? average height = DIVIDE(SUM(height), COUNT(id)); players born between 1990 and 1995 refers to birthday > = '1990-01-01 00:00:00' AND birthday < '1996-01-01 00:00:00';
orig_masked: What is the average [C122] of [T4] [C121] between [V3] and [V4]? average [C122] = DIVIDE(SUM([C122]), COUNT([C123])); [T4] [C121] between [V3] and [V4] refers to [C121] > = '[V1]' AND [C121] < '[V2]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C121]; [V2] is a value of the column [C121]; [V3] is a value of the column [C121]; [V4] is a value of the column [C121]
evidence: average height = DIVIDE(SUM(height (inches)), COUNT(id)); actors born between 1990 and 1995 refers to date of birth > = '1990-01-01 00:00:00' AND date of birth < '1996-01-01 00:00:00';
orig_evidence: average height = DIVIDE(SUM(height), COUNT(id)); players born between 1990 and 1995 refers to birthday > = '1990-01-01 00:00:00' AND birthday < '1996-01-01 00:00:00';
gold_sql: SELECT SUM("height (inches)") / COUNT(actorid) FROM actor WHERE SUBSTR("date of birth", 1, 4) BETWEEN '1990' AND '1995'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT SUM(height) / COUNT(id) FROM Player WHERE SUBSTR(birthday, 1, 4) BETWEEN '1990' AND '1995'

question: How many cars produced after 1975/12/31 have an abnormal horsepower? produced after 1975/12/31 refers to model_year > '1975'; normal horsepower refers to horsepower between 110 and 150; Should return the number of distinct cars.
orig_question: How many patients born after 1980/1/1 have an abnormal fibrinogen level? born after 1980/1/1 refers to Birthday > '1980-01-01'; normal fibrinogen level refers to FG between 150 and 450; Should return the number of distinct patients.
orig_masked: How many [T3] [C59] after [V2] have an [C26]? [C59] after [V2] refers to [C59] > '[V1]'; [C26] refers to [C26] between [V3] and [V4]; Should return the [C63] of distinct [T3].; [V1] is a value of the column [C59]; [V2] is a value of the column [C59]; [V3] is a value of the column [C26]; [V4] is a value of the column [C26]
evidence: produced after 1975/12/31 refers to model_year > '1975'; normal horsepower refers to horsepower between 110 and 150; Should return the number of distinct cars.
orig_evidence: born after 1980/1/1 refers to Birthday > '1980-01-01'; normal fibrinogen level refers to FG between 150 and 450; Should return the number of distinct patients.
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ID) FROM data AS T1 INNER JOIN production AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.horsepower <= 110 OR T1.horsepower >= 150 AND T2.model_year > '1975'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.FG <= 150 OR T2.FG >= 450 AND T1.Birthday > '1980-01-01'

question: What is the Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs characters villain name? Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs refers to movie_title = 'Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs'; villain name refers to villain;
orig_question: What is the ADO Den Haag team freedom of movement in the 1st two thirds of the pitch? ADO Den Haag refers to team_long_name = 'ADO Den Haag'; freedom of movement in the 1st two thirds of the pitch refers to buildUpPlayPositioningClass;
orig_masked: What is the [V1] [T6] [C179]? [V1] refers to [C173] = '[V1]'; [C179] refers to [C179];; [V1] is a value of the column [C173]

orig_evidence: ADO Den Haag refers to team_long_name = 'ADO Den Haag'; freedom of movement in the 1st two thirds of the pitch refers to buildUpPlayPositioningClass;

question: What are the addresses of the top 4 drivers with the highest weights? addresses refers to driver.address;highest weights refers to MAX(shipment.weight)
orig_question: What are the speed in which attacks are put together of the top 4 teams with the highest build Up Play Speed? speed in which attacks are put together refers to buildUpPlaySpeed;highest build up play speed refers to MAX(buildUpPlaySpeed)
orig_masked: What are the [C180] of the top [V1] [T6] with the highest [C180]? [C180] refers to [C180];highest [C180] refers to MAX([C180]); [V1] is a value of the column [C180]
evidence: addresses refers to driver.address;highest weights refers to MAX(shipment.weight)
orig_evidence: speed in which attacks are put together refers to buildUpPlaySpeed;highest build up play speed refers to MAX(buildUpPlaySpeed)
gold_sql: SELECT T1.address FROM driver AS T1 INNER JOIN shipment AS T2 ON T1.driver_id = T2.driver_id ORDER BY T2.weight DESC LIMIT 4
orig_gold_sql: SELECT t1.buildUpPlaySpeed FROM Team_Attributes AS t1 INNER JOIN Team AS t2 ON t1.team_api_id = t2.team_api_id ORDER BY t1.buildUpPlaySpeed ASC LIMIT 4

question: What is the supplier for Aniseed Syrup, Ikura and Genen Shouyu?  supplier refers to suppliername; Aniseed Syrup refers to productname = 'Aniseed Syrup'; Ikura refers to productname = 'Ikura'; Genen Shouyu refers to productname = 'Genen Shouyu';
orig_question: What is the publisher for Hawkman, Karate Kid and Speedy?  publisher refers to publisher_name; Hawkman refers to superhero_name = 'Hawkman'; Karate Kid refers to superhero_name = 'Karate Kid'; Speedy refers to superhero_name = 'Speedy';
orig_masked: What is the [T7] for [V2], [V1] and [V3]?  [T7] refers to [C15]; [V2] refers to [C28] = '[V2]'; [V1] refers to [C28] = '[V1]'; [V3] refers to [C28] = '[V3]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C28]; [V2] is a value of the column [C28]; [V3] is a value of the column [C28]

gold_sql: SELECT T1.suppliername FROM suppliers AS T1 INNER JOIN products AS T2 ON T1.supplierid = T2.supplierid WHERE T2.productname IN ('Aniseed Syrup', 'Ikura', 'Genen Shouyu')

question: What is Joe Mulligan's highest rating and what is it titled as? Joe Mulligan's refers to name = 'Joe Mulligan'; rating refers to MAX(episode.rating); titled refer to episode.title
orig_question: What is Ajax's highest chance creation passing score and what is it classified as? Ajax's refers to team_long_name = 'Ajax'; chance creation passing score refers to MAX(chanceCreationPassing); classified refer to chanceCreationPassingClass
orig_masked: What is [V1]'s highest [C184] and what is it [C185] as? [V1]'s refers to [C173] = '[V1]'; [C184] refers to MAX([C184]); [C185] refer to [C185]; [V1] is a value of the column [C173]
evidence: Joe Mulligan's refers to name = 'Joe Mulligan'; rating refers to MAX(episode.rating); titled refer to episode.title
orig_evidence: Ajax's refers to team_long_name = 'Ajax'; chance creation passing score refers to MAX(chanceCreationPassing); classified refer to chanceCreationPassingClass
gold_sql: SELECT T3.rating, T3.title from Person as T1 INNER JOIN Credit as T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id INNER JOIN Episode as T3 ON T2.episode_id = T3.episode_id WHERE T1.name = 'Joe Mulligan' ORDER BY T3.rating DESC LIMIT 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT t2.chanceCreationPassing, t2.chanceCreationPassingClass FROM Team AS t1 INNER JOIN Team_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.team_api_id = t2.team_api_id WHERE t1.team_long_name = 'Ajax' ORDER BY t2.chanceCreationPassing DESC LIMIT 1

question: What are the event names by the heaviest athlete to ever did an event? Identify their height. The larger the weight value, the heavier the person is, and vice versa; weight refers to the weight is not NULL
orig_question: What are the symptoms observed by the youngest patient to ever did a medical examination? Identify their diagnosis. The larger the birthday value, the younger the person is, and vice versa; symptoms observed refers to the symptoms is not NULL
orig_masked: What are the [C12] by the [C59] [T3] to ever did a [T1]? Identify their [C61]. The larger the [C59] value, the [C59] the [T3] is, and vice versa; [C12] refers to the [C12] is not NULL
evidence: The larger the weight value, the heavier the person is, and vice versa; weight refers to the weight is not NULL
orig_evidence: The larger the birthday value, the younger the person is, and vice versa; symptoms observed refers to the symptoms is not NULL
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.event_name FROM Event as T1 INNER JOIN competitor_event as T2 ON T1.id = T2.event_id WHERE T2.competitor_id in (SELECT id FROM Person WHERE weight IS NOT NULL ORDER BY weight DESC LIMIT 1)
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T2.Symptoms, T1.Diagnosis FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Examination AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.Symptoms IS NOT NULL ORDER BY T1.Birthday DESC LIMIT 1
